data.title: West Bancorporation Posts Strong 2025 Earnings, Raises Dividend
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

West Bancorporation ( (WTBA) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On January 29, 2026, West Bancorporation, Inc. reported that net income for 2025 rose to $32.6 million, or $1.92 per diluted share, up from $24.1 million, or $1.42 per diluted share, in 2024, driven by higher net interest income and improved net interest margin; fourth-quarter 2025 profit was $7.4 million, slightly above the prior-year quarter but down from the third quarter. The board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share payable February 25, 2026, as the company highlighted pristine credit quality with no nonaccrual or past-due loans at year-end, stronger deposit growth—particularly in core, non-brokered balances—an improved efficiency ratio and higher tangible common equity, while also recording a securities sale loss in November intended to enhance balance-sheet flexibility and long-term earnings capacity.

The most recent analyst rating on (WTBA) stock is a Buy with a $26.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on West Bancorporation stock, see the WTBA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WTBA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WTBA is a Outperform.

West Bancorporation’s overall stock score of 76 reflects strong financial performance and positive earnings call insights, supported by robust technical indicators and reasonable valuation. The company’s strategic initiatives and solid credit quality further enhance its growth outlook, despite minor challenges in deposit balances and client slowdowns.

To see Spark’s full report on WTBA stock, click here.

More about West Bancorporation

West Bancorporation, Inc., headquartered in West Des Moines, Iowa, is the parent of community bank West Bank, which has served customers since 1893. The bank focuses on lending, deposit and trust services for small- to medium-sized businesses and consumers, operating six offices in the Des Moines metropolitan area, one in Coralville, Iowa, and four in Minnesota in Rochester, Owatonna, Mankato and St. Cloud.

Average Trading Volume: 32,726

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $386.8M

data.title: Bread Financial Posts Strong 2025 Results, Raises Shareholder Returns
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bread Financial Holdings ( (BFH) ) has provided an update.

On January 29, 2026, Bread Financial reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results showing solid profitability and capital return alongside a stronger balance sheet. For 2025, the company generated $518 million in net income available to common stockholders, nearly doubled earnings per diluted share year over year, and lifted tangible book value per common share by 23% to $57.57, supported by higher net interest income, improved credit performance and lower credit-loss provisions. Management highlighted a resilient consumer backdrop, modest 3% growth in credit sales and stable partner relationships, with all top 10 programs renewed into at least 2028, while direct-to-consumer deposits rose 11% to $8.5 billion, now nearly half of total funding. The company also optimized its capital structure by retiring high-cost debt, issuing subordinated debt and preferred equity, and repurchasing $350 million of common stock in 2025, actions that contributed to credit rating upgrades and an improved outlook from major agencies. In conjunction with the earnings release, the board declared quarterly cash dividends payable on March 16, 2026 of $26.35 per share on its Series A preferred stock and $0.23 per share on common stock to shareholders of record on February 27, 2026, signaling continued commitment to shareholder returns while maintaining a common equity Tier 1 capital ratio of 13.0%.

The most recent analyst rating on (BFH) stock is a Sell with a $62.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bread Financial Holdings stock, see the BFH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BFH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BFH is a Outperform.

BFH scores well on strong cash generation and improving profitability, supported by constructive earnings-call signals (buyback, dividend raise, ratings upgrade). Valuation is also favorable with a low P/E. The main risks are contracting revenue/more volatile margins and an overbought technical setup that could pressure the stock in the near term.

To see Spark’s full report on BFH stock, click here.

More about Bread Financial Holdings

Bread Financial Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: BFH) is a tech-forward financial services company that provides simple, personalized payment, lending and saving solutions, including private-label and co-branded credit products, with a focus on partnering with brands across sectors such as travel, entertainment, technology and home to support consumer spending and loyalty programs.

data.title: Westwater Highlights Graphite Expansion at DealFlow Discovery Conference
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Westwater Resources ( (WWR) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 29, 2026, Westwater Resources’ Executive Chairman Terence Cryan was scheduled to present at the DealFlow Discovery Conference, using an updated investor presentation that outlines progress and plans for the company’s graphite business. The materials highlight that the Kellyton Graphite Plant is under construction with more than half of its $245 million capital cost already invested, Phase 1 capacity of 12,500 metric tons per year of battery-grade natural graphite nearly sold out under two offtake agreements, and plans to expand total capacity to 50,000 metric tons per year, supported by a qualification line and a patented purification process that has helped secure customers. The presentation also underscores the strategic significance of Westwater’s Coosa Graphite Deposit—covering about 42,000 acres, with drilling to date indicating 2.3 million short tons of natural graphite and a 2023 assessment pointing to a 22-year mine life at 99,000 short tons per year—reinforcing the company’s domestic, first-mover advantage as graphite demand accelerates with the growth of electric vehicles and energy storage.

The most recent analyst rating on (WWR) stock is a Hold with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Westwater Resources stock, see the WWR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WWR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WWR is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by very weak financial performance (no meaningful revenue, sizable losses, and continued cash burn), partially offset by a comparatively low-leverage balance sheet. Technicals are moderately supportive with price above several key moving averages and a constructive RSI, but momentum indicators are mixed. The latest earnings call was mixed as well: solid cash runway and project progress were tempered by increased financing risk following the Stellantis offtake termination.

To see Spark’s full report on WWR stock, click here.

More about Westwater Resources

Westwater Resources, listed on the NYSE American under the symbol WWR, is developing a vertically integrated graphite business in the United States, focused on producing battery-grade natural graphite for lithium-ion battery anodes. The company’s operations center on its Kellyton Graphite Plant in Kellyton, Alabama, and its Coosa Graphite Deposit in Coosa County, Alabama—one of the largest graphite deposits in the contiguous U.S.—positioning it as a potential first U.S.-based producer of battery-ready graphite serving electric vehicle, energy storage and national defense markets.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

AXIA Energia Pfd Class B ( ($AXIA.PR) ) has shared an update.

On January 23, 2026, Eletrobras reported that the Labor Court of Rio de Janeiro had revoked a preliminary injunction tied to two public civil actions brought by labor unions over alleged impacts on profit-sharing payments stemming from the capitalization of profit reserves and a share bonus approved at an extraordinary general meeting on December 19, 2025. On January 29, 2026, Hong Kong‑based Dreamland Limited, a foreign private issuer listed in the United States, reported unaudited interim condensed consolidated financial statements for the six months ended September 30, 2025, highlighting a sharp increase in total assets to HKD 90.98 million and shareholders’ equity to HKD 31.23 million, alongside a swing from retained earnings to an accumulated deficit of HKD 30.06 million and a rise in issued Class A ordinary shares from 28.66 million to 36 million.

data.title: Major Clene Insider Makes Back-to-Back Moves in the Market
data.description:
New insider activity at Clene ( (CLNN) ) has taken place on January 28, 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Significant shareholder Chidozie Ugwumba has recently reduced his position in Clene through two notable stock sales. In the first transaction, he sold 10,557 shares of Clene stock for a total value of $53,244. In a second transaction, he disposed of an additional 8,717 shares, generating proceeds of $42,800. Together, these back-to-back sales highlight a meaningful shift in Ugwumba’s holdings in the company.

Recent Updates on CLNN stock

In the last 24 hours, Clene’s stock has been driven by two main developments: a sizeable, milestone-linked financing and progress on its ALS program with the FDA. The company entered into a registered direct offering of over $28 million in three tranches, with an initial ~$6 million equity raise and Series A/B warrants whose exercise prices step up if CNM‑Au8 achieves key FDA milestones and if the share price trades above specified levels. Analysts’ recent stance and target assumptions are being influenced by this structure because it both extends Clene’s cash runway into at least the third quarter of 2026 and potentially through late 2026/early 2027, while also signaling future dilution and embedding expectations for successful NDA filing and approval. At the same time, the FDA granted Clene an in‑person Type C meeting this quarter to review biomarker data supporting a potential ALS NDA, including statistically significant reductions in NfL and GFAP and exploratory HEALEY trial findings linking CNM‑Au8 and IGFBP7 biomarker declines to reduced mortality risk. These regulatory and clinical signals are a key basis for analysts’ revised assumptions on Clene’s risk‑reward profile—supporting longer‑term opportunity in ALS—while their caution is grounded in the company’s weak current financials, ongoing cash burn, and bearish technicals, which temper expectations despite the biomarker and regulatory momentum.

Spark’s Take on CLNN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CLNN is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by very weak financial performance (minimal and declining revenue, large ongoing losses, negative equity and continued cash burn). Technicals also reflect a bearish trend with weak momentum. Offsetting factors include positive corporate developments (financing that extends runway and FDA engagement around biomarker-driven regulatory strategy), but they do not yet resolve the underlying financial risk.

data.title: Contrasting Insider Moves Rock Live Oak Bancshares Leadership Roster
data.description:
New insider activity at Live Oak Bancshares ( (LOB) ) has taken place on January 28, 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Live Oak Bancshares has seen notable insider activity, with Chief Experience Officer Courtney Spencer selling 9,700 shares of company stock in a transaction valued at $378,300, while Chief Risk Officer Ewa Maria Stasiowska moved in the opposite direction by purchasing 2,595 shares worth $99,985. These contrasting trades, involving hundreds of thousands of dollars in total, highlight differing insider positions within the company’s leadership ranks.

Recent Updates on LOB stock

Over the last day, sentiment around Live Oak Bancshares has been driven mainly by its stronger‑than‑expected Q4 results and upbeat outlook, which led at least one analyst (Keefe Bruyette) to lift its valuation expectations for the stock. The earnings call highlighted record loan production, double‑digit loan and revenue growth, and a sharp jump in profitability and EPS driven by operating leverage, as well as resilient net interest income and margin despite recent Fed cuts. Management also emphasized rapid progress in building low‑cost deposits and business checking relationships, growing cross‑sell (more customers holding both loans and deposits), and the scaling of its Live Oak Express SBA platform, all of which support a structurally better funding mix and fee income profile. While there are acknowledged headwinds—including rising nonaccrual loans in the SBA portfolio, near‑term margin pressure from timing mismatches in loan and deposit repricing after rate cuts, and some one‑time expense items—credit trends are described as stabilizing and comparatively strong versus peers. The combination of record growth, margin resilience, a more deposit‑rich balance sheet, and management’s guidance for continued expansion in net interest income and manageable credit costs appears to be the key rationale behind recent positive adjustments to analysts’ expectations, even as investors remain mindful of short‑term credit and margin risks.

Spark’s Take on LOB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LOB is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid fundamentals with a strong balance sheet but weaker cash flow and profitability trends. Technicals add support with a clear uptrend and positive momentum, while valuation is only average due to a higher P/E and low yield. The latest earnings call was constructive on growth and longer-term margin trajectory, tempered by near-term NIM compression and elevated nonaccruals.

Recent insider activity at Ambiq Micro, Inc. has drawn attention as two top executives executed notable stock sales. Director Joseph A. Tautges sold 7,800 shares of Ambiq Micro, Inc. stock in a transaction valued at $241,800. In a separate move, CTO Scott McLean Hanson disposed of 10,000 shares of the company’s stock, with the sale totaling $310,000. Recent Updates on AMBQ stock

In the last 24 hours, Ambiq Micro, Inc. saw attention centered on its enlarged equity offering and ongoing strategic developments rather than significant new price-target revisions. The company upsized its follow-on stock offering to about 2.68 million shares, with the deal priced below the prior close of $32.70, a dilutive move that can pressure the share price near term and is likely a key driver of recent trading. Most shares in the deal are being sold by the company to raise capital, while a smaller block is being sold by existing holders, from which Ambiq will receive no proceeds—this mix highlights both balance-sheet funding needs and some shareholder liquidity. Recent analyst price-target reductions referenced in the articles stem from earlier factors: despite reporting strong Q3 2025 revenue, Ambiq posted pro-forma losses and guided to another loss for Q4, underscoring ongoing profitability and cash-flow challenges; at the same time, macro and sector conditions in semis and edge AI appear to have tempered expectations for valuation multiples. Offsetting some of this, Ambiq’s launch of the Atomiq AI system-on-chip and expanding board with leadership expert Dr. Bernard Banks support a longer-term growth and governance narrative, which helps explain why ratings have trended toward neutral rather than outright negative even as targets have been trimmed.

data.title: Top Executive Makes Notable Move With Midland States Bancorp Stock
data.description:
New insider activity at Midland States Bancrop ( (MSBI) ) has taken place on January 28, 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Senior Vice President and Chief Operating Officer Kyle Owen Mooney has sold 2,808 shares of Midland States Bancorp stock, with the total value of the transaction amounting to $63,657. This insider sale provides a notable data point for investors tracking executive trading activity at Midland States Bancorp and may influence how some shareholders view the company’s current valuation and management’s outlook.

Recent Updates on MSBI stock

Over the last 24 hours, Midland States Bancorp (MSBI) has seen multiple analysts lift their price targets following the company’s Q4 2025 report and strategic actions, even though the quarter showed a headline net loss and an earnings miss versus consensus. DA Davidson cited disappointment with the Q4 results but based its revised outlook on the bank’s ongoing shift back to a core community banking model, with an emphasis on more consistent earnings and better credit stability that could make results cleaner by 2026. Piper Sandler’s change was driven by a belief that the elevated Q4 credit costs, while again worse than expected, should ease meaningfully after Midland sold the bulk of its historically higher-loss equipment finance portfolio and strengthened its credit culture and underwriting standards over the past year. Keefe Bruyette also nudged expectations higher in the context of these de-risking steps. Underpinning these moves, Midland’s update highlighted that 2025 was focused on credit cleanup and risk reduction, including the exit from most equipment finance exposure, improved nonperforming asset metrics, stronger capital ratios, and steady growth in core Community Bank lending and a record quarter in wealth management, all of which support a more favorable longer-term risk/return profile despite current-period losses and profitability pressure. The articles did not provide specific details on MSBI’s share price movement over the last 24 hours, but the combination of credit de-risking, capital strengthening, and refocus on core community banking and wealth management clearly framed the rationale behind the recent analyst target revisions.

Spark’s Take on MSBI Stock

Recent Updates on UPS stock

In the last 24 hours, sentiment around United Parcel Service has been shaped by its detailed Q4 and 2025 earnings discussion, which framed UPS as a company in a difficult but deliberate transition. The call highlighted that, despite significant volume declines and international trade headwinds, UPS is protecting profitability through stronger pricing, a shift toward higher‑yield SMB and B2B customers, and substantial cost‑cutting and automation initiatives. Management emphasized that margins will be pressured in the first half of 2026 because Amazon volumes are rolling off faster than fixed costs can be removed, because of transition expenses from outsourcing certain lower‑yield products, and because of temporary extra aircraft lease costs tied to grounding and retiring its MD‑11 fleet. At the same time, UPS outlined multi‑billion‑dollar cost‑savings plans, a more automated and smaller network, and growing digital and healthcare logistics businesses, arguing these structural changes should support a margin and earnings rebound beyond 2026. Any analyst price‑target revisions discussed in the last day are therefore being driven less by near‑term EPS growth and more by this mix of short‑term margin compression, macro and trade uncertainty, and the potential for longer‑term upside if UPS executes on its network reconfiguration, automation rollout, and customer mix strategy as planned.

Spark’s Take on UPS Stock
